title: [Docs]: Document advanced JetStream topology considerations
problem: The roadmap names stream mirrors, sources, subject transforms, republish behavior, stream compression, placement, and metadata guidance. These are JetStream capabilities that can affect how messages reach nats-sinks, but they are not core sink features. Users still need guidance about how these topology choices interact with event custody and idempotency.
proposal: Add documentation that explains how advanced JetStream topology features may affect nats-sinks deployments. The page should describe considerations, risks, and questions to ask rather than claiming direct management support for every JetStream capability.
users: NATS architects, platform operators, and maintainers answering deployment-design questions.
delivery_semantics: Topology choices can alter subjects, stream names, sequence behavior, and replay paths. Documentation must explain how idempotency strategies should be selected when stream or subject transformations are present.
security: Topology documentation must avoid private deployment examples and must warn that transformed subjects and metadata may still reveal sensitive operational structure.
acceptance: Mirrors, sources, transforms, republish, compression, placement, and metadata are discussed.
Idempotency and stream sequence implications are explained.
Unsupported management behavior is clearly separated from guidance.
README or operations docs link to the page.
tests: Run markdown link checks, mkdocs builds, and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/nats-feature-gap-analysis.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/operations.md, docs/idempotency.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after documentation ships in a release. Include release tag, pages changed, checks run, and follow-up implementation issues if any.
